commit.message:
Use dedicated http.Client with timeout instead of http.DefaultClient (#154)

* Initial plan

* Use dedicated http.Client with timeout instead of http.DefaultClient

- Add DefaultHTTPTimeout = 30s constant to client.go
- Add httpTimeout field to clientConfig, parsed from UPTIME_ROBOT_HTTP_TIMEOUT env var
- Add httpClient *http.Client field to Client struct
- Initialize dedicated httpClient with configured timeout in NewClient()
- Replace http.DefaultClient.Do() with c.httpClient.Do() in doWithRetry()
- Add nil guard in doWithRetry for backward-compat with directly-constructed test Clients
- Add TestNewClient_HasHTTPClientWithTimeout and TestDoWithRetry_HTTPClientTimeout tests

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/26d6efd9-1818-41b6-b65c-e45381338779

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tighten timeout test threshold to 1s (was 5s)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/26d6efd9-1818-41b6-b65c-e45381338779

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden HTTP client: log nil-guard fallback, add env var test coverage

Move the httpClient nil-guard above the retry loop so a single fallback
client is reused across attempts, and emit a stderr warning when it fires
to surface misconfigurations. Add tests for UPTIME_ROBOT_HTTP_TIMEOUT
env var override (valid and invalid values) and fix test isolation by
calling resetClientConfig() to prevent sync.Once cross-contamination.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Joel Pinder <joel.pinder@adarga.ai>

commit.message:
Add install and uninstall scripts

Install script (scripts/install_service.sh):
- Installs system deps (.NET 10 SDK, cmake, ffmpeg)
- Prompts user to select a Whisper model (tiny.en through large-v3)
- Clones and builds whisper.cpp
- Downloads the selected model
- Updates appsettings.json with correct paths
- Builds the .NET server
- Configures openscanner-whisper systemd service
- Supports --deps-only and --port=NNNN flags

Uninstall script (scripts/uninstall_service.sh):
- Stops, disables, and removes the systemd service

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: auto-create .gitignore before commits, switch to inline test workflow

Two critical fixes:
1. LocalWorkspace.CommitAsync() now calls EnsureGitignoreAsync() before
   staging files. This creates a standard .NET gitignore (bin/obj/node_modules
   etc.) if one doesn't exist, preventing build artifacts from being committed.
   This was the root cause of PR conflicts with bin/obj files.

2. Switched TestWorkflow config to 'inline' so the TE writes tests directly
   on the engineer's PR branch instead of creating separate test PRs.
   This eliminates the proliferation of stale test PRs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Drop all-NaN metric rows from heatmap

have zero variance across all teams, producing undefined z-scores.
These now get filtered out instead of displaying as NaN rows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add l_tls.h — freestanding TLS/HTTPS client

Phase 1: Windows SChannel implementation (zero external dependencies)
- l_tls_init/connect/send/recv/recv_byte/close/cleanup API
- Up to 8 simultaneous TLS connections (handle-based pool)
- TLS 1.2 + 1.3 via OS-native SChannel (secur32.dll)
- Auto DNS resolution via l_resolve, manual port-to-string
- Uses HeapAlloc for encrypt buffers, l_memset/l_memcpy throughout
- Proper TLS shutdown notification on close

Phase 2: Linux/WASI stubs (return -1, future BearSSL integration)
- L_TLS_AVAILABLE macro for compile-time platform detection
- Stub functions compile cleanly on all 7 targets

Tests: test_tls.c (12 assertions — init/cleanup, invalid handles, availability)
Example: https_get.c — fetch HTTPS pages from the command line
Docs: README updated with header table, quick start, function reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Extract cmd_verify_execution into verify_execution module

Break the 1063-line cmd_verify_execution function into focused phase
functions in a new verify_execution module:

- VerifyContext: bundles all long-lived resources (archive, CDP, LedgerManager,
  TempDir guards)
- VerifyStats: accumulator counters with derived Default
- setup(): phases 1-4 (config, clients, state download, LedgerManager init)
- run_verification_loop(): phase 5 (checkpoint iteration)
- verify_single_ledger(): per-ledger CDP fetch, close_ledger, comparison
- print_summary(): phase 6 (verification + performance summaries)

Also moves 6 helper functions only used by verify_execution:
tx_result_code_name, print_op_diffs, print_all_ops,
print_header_field_diffs, print_tx_result_diffs, for_each_tx_meta_changes.

main.rs shrinks from 5687 to 4174 lines.

Closes #1437

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add finalizers to Record (#4)

* Add finalizers and DeletionTimestamp to Record

- Remove unused UID field from ObjectMeta
- Rename DeletedAt -> DeletionTimestamp (was never wired through storage)
- Add Finalizers []string to ObjectMeta and storage.Row
- Add DeletionTimestamp *time.Time to storage.Row
- Add IsTerminating() helper on ObjectMeta
- Add storage.ErrTerminating sentinel

This is the foundational layer for the finalizer state machine; service
and storage CRUD changes follow in subsequent commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Implement finalizer state machine

- SQLite: add finalizers/deletion_timestamp columns, add optimistic
  locking (resource_version in UPDATE WHERE clause)
- Postgres: add finalizers/deletion_timestamp to all CRUD queries,
  add migration 0003_finalizers.sql
- Service: Delete soft-deletes via optimistic-lock Update (race-safe),
  Update guards against adding finalizers to terminating records and
  auto-triggers hard-delete when last finalizer is removed
- Proto: add finalizers + deletion_timestamp fields to ObjectMeta
- Server: map new proto fields, map ErrTerminating → FailedPrecondition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clarify IsTerminating docstring and add unit tests

commit.message:
fix(e2e): fix all 17 E2E tests to pass against running server

Root causes fixed:
- Wrong room topology: replaced 'go north/south' with 'go down/up'
  (starting room reliquary-inn only has 'down' exit)
- Stale character collisions: character names now get random alpha
  suffix (e.g., Alicexmqp) so linkdead chars from prior tests
  don't pollute assertions
- Pattern matching: use dynamic names (alice.name) instead of
  hardcoded strings; match 'is.*here' to handle posture variants
  ('is standing here', 'is crouching here')
- Scoped assertions: 'disappears from look' test now checks only
  fresh messages after the second look command, not the full log

Files changed:
- test-fixture.ts: unique alpha suffix on character names
- movement.spec.ts: correct exits, dynamic names, scoped assertions
- follow.spec.ts: correct exits, dynamic names for follow/consent
- group.spec.ts: dynamic names for follow/group commands
- playwright.config.ts: serial execution (workers: 1)
- auth/routes.ts: bypass rate limiting in ALLOW_LOCAL_AUTH mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: cover MockFs::exists short-circuit branch in cleanup.rs

Add test mock_fs_exists_returns_true_for_dir to exercise the
`dirs.contains_key(path)` true branch of the `||` expression in
MockFs::exists (cleanup.rs:124). Previously only the false branch
(evaluate `files.contains`) was covered; the short-circuit path was
never hit because existing tests only inserted paths into `files`.

Branch coverage for cleanup.rs: 94.44% → 100%
Overall branch coverage: 93.45% → 93.50%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add static file serving - backend now serves index.html

Added static file middleware to serve frontend from backend:
- Serves index.html from /public folder
- Intelligently routes: API requests get JSON, browser gets HTML
- Root endpoint (/) now detects content type and responds accordingly
- All API endpoints (/api/*) still work as before

This allows:
- Frontend to be served from same backend (Render)
- OR frontend deployed separately (Netlify) still works
- Flexible deployment options

Created /public folder with index.html inside.

Fix: strip role mentions (<@&id>) not just user mentions

Discord sends role mentions when @mentioning by role name.
The old regex only handled <@id> and <@!id>.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Debug: log raw message content, loosen report coaches regex

Removed anchor/boundary from regex so it matches anywhere in content.
Added console logs to see exactly what Discord sends.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve alliance chart visual distinction with unique per-team styles

Each team now gets a unique combination of color, line width, dash
pattern, and marker shape. Red alliance uses warm hues (red, orange,
maroon) and blue alliance uses cool hues (blue, purple, cyan). Legend
groups separate the two alliances clearly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: move find_sibling_token to config_helpers module

Mypy in server CI rejects 'from provider.__init__ import' as it
registers __init__ as a separate module name. Moving to a dedicated
config_helpers module gives a clean 'from provider.config_helpers import'
path that the sync-to-fork sed rule rewrites correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.3.0: lifecycle completeness — cancel, reopen, recovery

Add task-cancel command (planned/ready/in_progress/in_review/blocked → cancelled)
Add task-reopen command (done/in_review/cancelled → in_progress)
Fix review-slice to accept cancelled tasks (isTerminalTaskStatus)
Create recovering-from-broken-state skill (6 recovery patterns)
Add error branches to lifecycle decision tree in using-mbscode
Fix executing-task-slices 'pause' reference → task-cancel
Update CLAUDE.md, PHILOSOPHY.md, README.md with new commands
Bump version to 0.3.0

Addresses 8 lifecycle gaps:
- g1 HIGH: No task cancellation
- g2 HIGH: No task abandonment/pause
- g3 HIGH: review-slice blocks on cancelled tasks
- g4 MEDIUM: No doc remediation workflow
- g5 MEDIUM: No recovery skill for broken state
- g6 MEDIUM: No in_review → in_progress transition
- g7 LOW: Decision tree missing error branches
- g8 LOW: No cross-task doc consistency check

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Drop Python 3.9 support, require Python 3.10+ (#36)

Raises the minimum supported Python version to 3.10 across the project
metadata and CI/CD workflows.

## Changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: Added `requires-python = ">=3.10"`
- **`ci.yml`**: Removed `"3.9"` from the test matrix
- **`cd.yml`**: Pinned `python-version` from `3.x` to `3.10`

Fix: upgrade coverage debug logging to Information level

The coverage check was using LogDebug which doesn't show in default log
config, making it impossible to diagnose why testing.coverage.met signal
wasn't firing. Added extra case for zero-session-tested state.

fix: GIF drag snapping + editable start/end inputs

Drag snapping fix (Timeline.vue):
- Move ALL pointer handling onto the track div itself instead of
  child handle elements. This eliminates the race condition where
  a pointerup on a handle caused a synthetic click event to fire
  on the track and reset the range back to 5s.
- Handle hit detection via HANDLE_HIT_PX (18px) proximity check in
  onTrackPointerDown — no child event listeners needed at all.
- Pointer capture on the TRACK element, so pointermove always arrives
  even when cursor moves outside the timeline bounds.
- Plain (non-reactive) dragMoved flag guards onTrackClick after a drag.
- Handle divs are now pointer-events-none (visual only).

Editable range inputs (GifControls.vue):
- Add start/end number inputs (step 0.1s) so users can type exact times
- Inputs are shown when a range is selected, above the quality buttons
- End input max is clamped to start + MAX_GIF_DURATION (10s)
- Duration badge shows current length and turns amber at the 10s limit
- Pass start, end, videoDuration props from editor.vue

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: address PR #3614 review comments

- _resolve_token: treat all refresh errors (including LoginFailed) as
  non-fatal when stored token is available — prevents transient Passport
  failures from blocking startup
- _send: mark disconnected and schedule reconnect on send failure
  instead of silently swallowing the error
- _find_sibling_token: return None instead of empty string for missing
  x_token
- _build_device_dict: remove hardcoded volume_info and volume_granularity
  since volume sync was removed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Playwright: use standard browser path, add node-based CLI install

The PlaywrightRunner's browser install was failing because:
1. The pwsh script approach was broken (missing DLL)
2. The custom browsers path didn't check the standard ms-playwright location

Fixes:
- GetPlaywrightBrowsersPath() now checks standard ms-playwright first
- EnsureBrowsersInstalledAsync() tries node-based CLI (most reliable)
- Added FindNodePlaywrightCli() to discover node/cli.js from build output

Tested: standalone Blazor app + Playwright test passes with screenshots
and video recording confirmed working.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
